zh.lifeSummary: 當Mathilde van Liedekerke van Gendt d'Alost de Boulogne於大約1140年出生在比利時福拉阿姆-布拉班特利德凱爾克時，她的父親Iwan van Aelst, Graaf van Aalst51歲，母親Lauretta van Vlaanderen19歲。她於大約1161年與Rasse de Gavre VII結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於從1190年到1224年。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mathilde van Liedekerke van Gendt d'Alost de Boulogne于大约1140年出生在比利时福拉阿姆-布拉班特利德凱爾克时，她的父亲Iwan van Aelst, Graaf van Aalst51岁，母亲Lauretta van Vlaanderen19岁。她于大约1161年与Rasse de Gavre VII结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于从1190年到1224年。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). nameMeanings.name: Gendt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Johannes, Katharina.
Danish: from the personal name Bendt, Danish form of Benedict .
German: habitational name from a place so named near Hanover.

variants.name: Benedict
meanings: English, German, and Dutch: from the personal name Benedict, from Latin Benedictus ‘blessed’. This owed its popularity in the Middle Ages chiefly to Saint Benedict of Norcia (c. 480–550), who founded the Benedictine order of monks at Monte Cassino and wrote a monastic rule that formed a model for all subsequent rules. No doubt the meaning of the Latin word also contributed to its popularity as a personal name, especially in Romance countries. Occasionally the English surname may derive from Latin benedicite ‘bless (you)’, perhaps given as a nickname to an habitual user of the expression. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed the German variant Benedikt and many cognates from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Benedek , Slovenian Benedik (see Benedick ), and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Italian Benedetti .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mathilde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Mechtild
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Matilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mechtilde
meanings: from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’.

en.lifeSummary: When Mathilde van Liedekerke van Gendt d'Alost de Boulogne was born about 1140, in Liedekerke, Flemish Brabant, Belgium, her father, Iwan van Aelst, Graaf van Aalst, was 51 and her mother, Lauretta van Vlaanderen, was 19. She married Rasse de Gavre VII about 1161. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died from 1190 to 1224.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mathilde van Liedekerke van Gendt d'Alost de Boulogne est née environ 1140, à Liedekerke, Brabant Flamand, Belgique, son père, Iwan van Aelst, Graaf van Aalst, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Lauretta van Vlaanderen, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Rasse de Gavre VII environ 1161. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée du 1190 au 1224.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mathilde van Liedekerke van Gendt d'Alost de Boulogne um 1140 in Liedekerke, Flämisch Brabant, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Iwan van Aelst, Graaf van Aalst 51 und ihre Mutter Lauretta van Vlaanderen 19. Sie hat um 1161 Rasse de Gavre VII geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb von 1190 bis 1224.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde van Liedekerke van Gendt d'Alost de Boulogne è nata il circa 1140 nel Liedekerke, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgio, sua padre, Iwan van Aelst, Graaf van Aalst, aveva 51 anni e sua madre, Lauretta van Vlaanderen, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato Rasse de Gavre VII circa 1161. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì da 1190 a 1224.

ja.lifeSummary: Mathilde van Liedekerke van Gendt d'Alost de Boulogneが約1140年にベルギーvラアムス-ブラバントリーデケルクで生まれた時，父Iwan van Aelst, Graaf van Aalstは51歳，母Lauretta van Vlaanderenは19歳であった。約1161年にRasse de Gavre VIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はから1190年まで1224年に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mathilde van Liedekerke van Gendt d'Alost de Boulogne(이)가 대략 1140년 벨기에 블라아스-브라반트 리데커크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Iwan van Aelst, Graaf van Aalst(은)는 51세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lauretta van Vlaanderen(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 대략 1161년 Rasse de Gavre VII(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1190년 부터 1224년 까지 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mathilde van Liedekerke van Gendt d'Alost de Boulogne nasceu aproximadamente 1140, em Liedekerke, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, seu pai, Iwan van Aelst, Graaf van Aalst, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Lauretta van Vlaanderen, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com Rasse de Gavre VII aproximadamente 1161. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu de 1190 a 1224.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mathilde van Liedekerke van Gendt d'Alost de Boulogne родилась около 1140 г. в местности лиедекирк, влаамс-брабант, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Iwan van Aelst, Graaf van Aalst было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Lauretta van Vlaanderen, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Rasse de Gavre VII около 1161 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла с 1190 по 1224.

sm.environ 1140–from 1190 to 1224: e tusa o le 1140–mai le 1190 e o'o I le 1224
sm.from 1190 to 1224: mai le 1190 e o'o I le 1224

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mathilde van Liedekerke van Gendt d'Alost de Boulogne nació aproximadamente 1140, en Liedekerke, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, su papá, Iwan van Aelst, Graaf van Aalst, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Lauretta van Vlaanderen, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Rasse de Gavre VII aproximadamente 1161. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió desde 1190 hasta 1224.
